title: Koelreuteria elegans

It is native to Asia, and its classification reflects its evolutionary history within this family. Koelreuteria elegans is further classified as a member of the genus Koelreuteria, which is characterized by its distinct flowers and seed pods. This classification helps scientists understand the plant's relationships with other species and its place within the broader plant kingdom.

side_effects: Koelreuteria elegans, a plant commonly known as the golden rain tree, has potential side effects due to the toxicity of its seeds and leaves. intro: Koelreuteria elegans, also known as Goldenrain tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its attractive golden-yellow flowers and fragrant seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, providing potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
